title: eriogonum lonchophyllum var. intermontanum
intro: Eriogonum lonchophyllum var. intermontanum, also known as the mountain buckwheat or intermountain buckwheat, is a perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of the western United States. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Eriogonum lonchophyllum var. intermontanum include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide relief from coughs and colds. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, teas, or tinctures made from its leaves and stems, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
